prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
English: The silence between us said more than any argument could have.
French: Le silence qui nous séparait a dit plus que n'importe quelle argumentation ne le pouvait. (Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was translated as "que n'importe quelle argumentation ne le pouvait" which means "than any argument could.")
Japanese: 我々の間の静けさは、どのような議論でもできる限りのことを言っていた。 (Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was translated as "どのような議論でもできる限りのことを言っていた" which means "than any argument could.")
Arabic: الصمت بيننا قال أكثر من أي تناقض يمكن أن يقوله. (Note: The phrase "more than any argument could have" was translated as "أكثر من أي تناقض يمكن أن يقوله" which means "than any argument could.")
